channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Assessing (and Not Assessing) the Threats to the United States
item.description: The U.S. intelligence community’s latest global threat assessment focuses heavily on Iran.

item.title: Trump Praises Japan’s Takaichi for ‘Stepping Up’ in Iran War
item.description: But Tokyo remains wary of offering direct military support in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump Praises Takaichi for Supporting Iran War
item.description: But Tokyo remains wary of offering direct military support in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Ukraine Wants to Cash in on Iran’s Drone Threat
item.description: Kyiv has valuable counterdrone expertise and technology it can offer Gulf countries. But there are hurdles.

item.title: Israel Attacking Iran’s Energy ‘Wasn’t a Surprise’ to the Trump Administration
item.description: Israel’s top diplomat in New York City says strikes like the one on Iran’s South Pars gas field were “part of the plan.”

item.title: Trump Is Burying His Own Security Strategy
item.description: US President Donald Trump has leapt feet first into a major new Middle East war, the objectives of which are changing by the day. As the conflict escalates, the endgame scenarios are growing increasingly bleak and complicated, making a mockery of the spirit and letter of Trump's three-month-old National Security Strategy.

item.title: The U.S. and Israel Aren’t Fighting the Same War
item.description: It would be a mistake for Trump to defer to Netanyahu’s regime change fantasies.

item.title: Tucker Carlson on whether Donald Trump has betrayed his base
item.description: And why MAGA must look toward Europe

item.title: Turkmenistan Dismisses 2 Top US-Based Diplomats After Decades in Office
item.description: After 25 and 32 years, respectively, Meret Orazov and Aksoltan Ataeva are out of their U.S.-based diplomatic jobs, allegedly for failing to score Turkmenistan an invite to the Board of Peace.

item.title: South Korea’s Arms Exports Are Now Involved in the Iran War
item.description: Seoul’s ballooning defense industry has always had a blind spot: the operational and political consequences of its weapons systems being put to use.

item.title: David vs Goliath: How Vanuatu Is Standing up to the US
item.description: For small, climate-vulnerable states, climate change is not a bargaining chip but an existential priority.

item.title: How the Iran war is hurting American farmers
item.description: Surging fertiliser prices are a blow to a limping industry

item.title: Is an obsession with immigration leaving America exposed?
item.description: The Iran war raises questions about the country’s counterterrorism capabilities

item.title: The weekly cartoon
Business
Politics
Israel contemplates a ground invasion of Lebanon
item.description: That could wreck Lebanon’s chances of taking on Hizbullah itself

item.title: A shake-up at Africa’s spikiest media group
item.description: Can the new owner preserve its independent spirit?

item.title: The war in eastern Congo is escalating far from view
item.description: Fighting in South Kivu is a disaster for civilians and may threaten the copper belt

item.title: Africa after aid is more resilient than you might think
item.description: But more needs to be done to ensure a prosperous future

item.title: Gas will not be killed off by renewables any time soon
item.description: But there are ways to rely less on it

item.title: Lebanon’s leaders must take on Hizbullah
item.description: And Israel must not play the spoiler

item.title: Life in Myanmar’s biggest city is increasingly grim
item.description: A new rubber-stamp parliament will solve nothing

item.title: Why China’s fight on air pollution has slowed
item.description: Officials are now trying to take it to the next stage

item.title: Public opinion in China is hardening on America and Taiwan
item.description: A rare poll shows the views of ordinary Chinese are changing

item.title: The future of Africa will be shaped by investment rather than aid
item.description: Foreign and African firms are giving the continent reasons for hope

item.title: Viktor Orban’s pro-natalist policies are not working
item.description: Despite lavish spending on mothers, Hungary’s fertility rate is falling again

item.title: The Iran war is forcing Europe to confront its energy problem
item.description: High power prices are putting the continent’s economy at risk

item.title: The secrets to a good employee survey
item.description: Likert or not

item.title: The Iran war casts a shadow over BASF’s nascent revival
item.description: The turnaround of the German chemicals giant is still a work in progress

item.title: Cuba’s broken economy leaves it at Donald Trump’s mercy
item.description: He seems more interested in creating a client state than a free Cuba

item.title: American farmers are feeling the squeeze
item.description: Surging fertiliser prices are a blow to a limping industry

item.title: Iran Can Afford America’s Economic Warfare
item.description: The United States is increasing its pressure, but Tehran can still shift to a war economy.

item.title: Get Ready for a Weaker but Nastier Iran
item.description: What the war’s most likely outcome means for the United States and the Middle East.

item.title: What the Iran Crisis Means for Middle Powers
item.description: For solidarity among the world's middle powers to matter, it must be used to create a new system of states that are governed by the rule of law and committed to democratic norms and principles. The alternative is a world governed by power alone, where dependence is weaponized, and public consent becomes an inconvenience.

item.title: Taiwan’s New Southbound 2.0: Rewiring the Indo-Pacific Beyond China
item.description: Taiwan intends to deepen and broaden its partnerships not only with New Southbound countries, but also with democratic partners across the world.

item.title: Indian Courts Must Be Mindful of Judicial Limits in Electoral Matters
item.description: The Supreme Court’s intervention in the West Bengal SIR dispute must not be so deep as to adversely affect the electoral process.

item.title: Deonar Dumping Ground Is the Key to Unlock Mumbai’s Climate Goals
item.description: Waste-to-energy technologies will only exacerbate the toxic emissions from the giant landfill. There’s another way.

item.title: There is plenty of scope for the Iran war to intensify
item.description: But no obvious way to bring it to an end

item.title: Will AGI Really Be the “Last Invention”?
item.description: Listen to leading voices in Silicon Valley, and you might come to believe that “solving intelligence” is sufficient to “solve everything else.” But as seductive as this claim about AI’s potential may be, it rests on a number of assumptions that do not withstand scrutiny.

item.title: Iran-Israel-US War Tests Japan’s Evolving Appetite for Collective Self-Defense
item.description: Can Japan send the SDF to Hormuz? The key will be how Tokyo classifies the current situation – but that is complicated by the conflict’s origin in a preemptive U.S. attack.

item.title: Are Uzbeks Really Happy?
item.description: By some measures, Uzbeks appear relatively content; by others, their sense of well-being is more restrained.

item.title: A deadly strike in Kabul could have big knock-on effects
item.description: The war between Afghanistan and Pakistan is growing ever more lethal

item.title: Why AI has not yet upset India’s IT industry
item.description: Deploying the technology is the real world is proving tricky

item.title: Panicked Indians are scrambling to buy gas
item.description: War over Iran threatens the fuel that powers hundreds of millions of stoves

item.title: America may be a petrostate. But the energy shock still hurts
item.description: And further angers economically frustrated citizens

item.title: Which country is the biggest loser from the energy shock?
item.description: We rank the poor world’s exposure and buffers

item.title: The new economics of sex work
item.description: As the sex economy grows, it deserves serious analysis

item.title: War in Iran is making Donald Trump weaker—and angrier
item.description: By diminishing the president’s political superpowers, his reckless campaign may make him more dangerous

item.title: Does Donald Trump even care about the midterms?
item.description: He is more focused on dominating his party, and on his place in history

item.title: The Gulf War and Flawed Diplomacy Fuel LPG Crisis in India
item.description: While India’s pro-U.S.-Israel position in the war has not drawn public ire, the LPG shortage is becoming an issue in upcoming assembly elections.

item.title: Can Nepal’s Young Leaders Overcome Old Constraints?
item.description: While Nepal’s mostly young lawmakers offer hope, youth does not necessarily mean delivery. The old vested interests will fight to preserve their privileges.

item.title: Building the Energy Resilience ASEAN+3 Needs
item.description: Climate shocks, rapid technological change, and geopolitical turmoil are adding to the pressure on energy systems across Southeast Asia, underscoring the importance of enhancing resilience. Strengthening energy resilience is not only an energy-policy priority; it is a macroeconomic imperative.

item.title: Ukraine Is Making Home-Brew Long-Range Missiles
item.description: Faced with a shortage of weapons to hit Russia, Ukrainians have developed their own.

item.title: It’s Official: Trump’s Tariffs Have Failed
item.description: Americans are paying, reindustrialization isn’t happening, and China profits.

item.title: Hating Ukraine Is Viktor Orban’s Reelection Strategy
item.description: Can the Hungarian leader’s foreign-policy distractions beat the ascendant Peter Magyar?

item.title: Habermas and the World We Have Lost
item.description: With his death at 96, Jürgen Habermas, a titan of postwar political philosophy leaves behind a world that seems to be dismantling everything he defended, both as a scholar and as an engaged public intellectual. He and the approach he championed will be missed.

item.title: Thai Parliament Reconfirms Anutin Charnvirakul as Prime Minister
item.description: The 59-year-old now faces the challenge of dealing with the domestic impacts of the oil price shock triggered by the war in Iran.

item.title: Can Mexico Avoid a Confrontation With the United States?
item.description: How Sheinbaum can counter Trump’s threats.

item.title: The Stunning Failure of Iranian Deterrence
item.description: And why it augurs a more dangerous world.

item.title: Why Bhumjaithai’s Grip on Power is More Fragile Than It Looks
item.description: Thailand's blue party won last month's election and secured the establishment's blessing. But it has not won Bangkok – and that may prove decisive down the road.

item.title: Why Indonesia’s Proposed Aircraft Carrier Acquisition Makes Sense
item.description: The Giuseppe Garibaldi could enhance the operational flexibility of the Indonesian Navy, while remaining consistent with the country's overall defense posture.

item.title: Confusion Reigns After Malaysian Minister Declares US Trade Agreement ‘Null and Void’
item.description: If confirmed, Malaysia would become the first country to withdraw from a "reciprocal" trade deal with the Trump administration.

item.title: How the Iran war is hurting Donald Trump
item.description: An unpopular conflict and costly fuel could hobble his presidency
